assumptions: Structural rules are read from the Level 2 sector-coupling scaffold.
Relative bounds are evaluated on the finite protected complement.
No empirical mass, CKM, PMNS, residual, or prediction-ledger data are used.
banded_support_certificate: banded after mode-block ordering because nonzero Dirac couplings preserve k, j, q, and chirality
compactness_diagnostic: relative-bound candidate only; no compactness theorem because baseline coupling does not strongly decay with action
finite_rank_certificate: finite rank at fixed k_max; not finite-rank certified in the infinite-basis limit
limitations: The structured bound is finite-basis unless made uniform in k_max.
The full zero-mode/complement proof remains open.
This audit does not prove the full H_T theorem.

assumptions: The base operator is positive on the protected finite complement.
The sector perturbation is symmetric on the audited finite complement.
evidence: Computed finite generalized relative bound ||B^{-1/2} K B^{-1/2}||.
Compared structured lower bound (1-a_K) lambda_1(B)-b_K to the required Dirac lower bound.
limitations: The certificate is finite-basis unless the same relative bound is proven uniformly as k_max grows.
It does not prove the full H_T theorem or the zero-mode/complement decomposition.
relative_certificate.name: structured_sector_relative_bound

limitations: After squaring, D^dagger D includes same-sector diagonal contributions.
structural_rules.statement: Dirac-level coupling connects distinct charged sectors only.

evidence: Every nonzero Dirac-level sector pair has matching k, j, q, and chirality.
limitations: This is a Level 2 selection rule, not a full action theorem.
structural_rules.statement: Dirac-level coupling preserves k, j, q, and chirality.
Sector coupling is sparse in the finite Dirac basis.

evidence: First 3 rows and columns vanish in both Dirac-level and squared perturbation blocks.
limitations: The protected zero-mode block is finite-basis inserted/projected.
structural_rules.statement: Sector coupling vanishes on the protected zero-mode coordinate block.

evidence: Nonzero entries preserve k, j, and chirality while changing sector.
limitations: The current matrix storage order is sector-major.
structural_rules.statement: Coupling is block-banded after reordering by (k,j,chirality), but not in the raw sector-major basis.

limitations: No k_max-uniform finite-rank certificate is available.
structural_rules.statement: Coupling is finite rank at fixed k_max but rank grows with basis size.
structural_rules.value: finite-basis finite-rank; infinite-basis finite-rank not certified

limitations: UNIFORM_BOUND_CANDIDATE is finite truncation evidence, not a proof of the infinite-basis theorem.
The full H_T theorem remains open until zero-mode/complement and infinite-basis limits are certified.
